symptoms.symptom_name: Reproducible intermittent claudication (calf cramp after ~50 m, relieved by rest)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reproducible exertional calf pain relieved within minutes of rest is the defining symptom of claudication from PAD.
Relief by simply standing/resting rather than sitting/spinal flexion favours vascular over neurogenic claudication.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute worsening of claudication over one week

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Rapid deterioration raises concern for progression toward a limb-threatening state and warrants prompt assessment and safety-netting.

symptoms.symptom_name: No rest pain, ulceration or acute pallor/coldness/numbness (no acute limb ischaemia)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of rest pain, tissue loss and the 6 Ps indicates a currently viable limb suitable for urgent outpatient management.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bilateral exertional pain without unilateral swelling/erythema/cord (argues against DVT)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bilateral, strictly exertional pain without swelling is not the pattern of a unilateral DVT.

symptoms.symptom_name: Very high cardiovascular risk profile (untreated, sedentary, processed diet, avoided care)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Untreated severe hyperlipidaemia and lifestyle risk drive the systemic atherosclerosis underlying PAD.

symptoms.symptom_name: Carotid bruits, weak femoral pulses, absent pedal pulses, and tendon xanthomas/xanthelasma/corneal arcus

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absent pedal pulses with bruits confirm PAD, and the lipid stigmata confirm the severe hyperlipidaemia driving it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Reduced ankle-brachial index and severely elevated LDL on fasting bloods

investigation_details.investigation_name: Ankle-brachial index and fasting lipid profile
investigation_details.expected_result: ABI <0.9 confirming PAD and markedly elevated LDL cholesterol
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: PAD severity and lipid targets remain unquantified and treatment cannot be optimised.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A reduced ABI confirms flow-limiting peripheral arterial disease.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Stable symptomatic peripheral arterial disease from severe hyperlipidaemia
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Reproducible claudication with a viable limb (no rest pain/6 Ps), no DVT features, and a very high untreated cardiovascular risk profile together indicate stable PAD needing prompt outpatient vascular assessment and aggressive risk-factor treatment, not emergency surgery.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms and quantifies PAD; requires in-person assessment and cannot be done over telehealth.
Quantifies the severe hyperlipidaemia and screens for diabetes to guide statin and risk-factor therapy.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Resting 12-lead ECG and cardiovascular risk assessment
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Given widespread atherosclerosis, assess for coexisting coronary disease and overall risk.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency department presentation for a viable limb with stable exertional claudication
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Unnecessary over-triage for a viable limb; urgent outpatient vascular assessment is appropriate, reserving ED for acute limb ischaemia features.
